{"body":"Hello, 

 For the BP refactoring, we want to untangle multi-cellular organism processes from cellular processes. 
For the 'developmental process' branch, we propose to move 'cellular developmental process' under 'cellular process' and rename 'developmental process' -> 'multi-cellular organism developmental process' 

@thomaspd 

Thanks, Pascale","comments":[{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5dwOsb","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Proposed changes: 

- [ ] move 'GO:0048869 cellular developmental process' to 'is_a 'GO:0009987 cellular process'

- [ ] CREATE NEW TERM 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process' under which to move organism-level processes (embryonic development?? see https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Animal_embryonic_development) 

Review children of developmental process: 
- [ ] 'GO:0048532 anatomical structure arrangement' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] 'GO:0048856 anatomical structure development' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] 'GO:0048646 anatomical structure formation involved in morphogenesis' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] 'GO:0009653 anatomical structure morphogenesis' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] 'GO:0060033 anatomical structure regression' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] GO:0043697 cell dedifferentiation > move under GO:0048869 cellular developmental process
- [ ] GO:0043696 dedifferentiation > obsolete, unnecessary grouping class
- [ ] GO:0031128 developmental induction' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
 - [ ] GO:0021700 developmental maturation' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] GO:0048
...
</persisted-output>

{"body":"Hello, 

 For the BP refactoring, we want to untangle multi-cellular organism processes from cellular processes. 
For the 'developmental process' branch, we propose to move 'cellular developmental process' under 'cellular process' and rename 'developmental process' -> 'multi-cellular organism developmental process' 

@thomaspd 

Thanks, Pascale","comments":[{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5dwOsb","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Proposed changes: 

- [ ] move 'GO:0048869 cellular developmental process' to 'is_a 'GO:0009987 cellular process'

- [ ] CREATE NEW TERM 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process' under which to move organism-level processes (embryonic development?? see https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Animal_embryonic_development) 

Review children of developmental process: 
- [ ] 'GO:0048532 anatomical structure arrangement' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] 'GO:0048856 anatomical structure development' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] 'GO:0048646 anatomical structure formation involved in morphogenesis' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] 'GO:0009653 anatomical structure morphogenesis' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] 'GO:0060033 anatomical structure regression' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] GO:0043697 cell dedifferentiation > move under GO:0048869 cellular developmental process
- [ ] GO:0043696 dedifferentiation > obsolete, unnecessary grouping class
- [ ] GO:0031128 developmental induction' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
 - [ ] GO:0021700 developmental maturation' > move under new term 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process'
- [ ] GO:0048757 'pigment granule maturation'  >  not a developmental process (remove is_a developmental maturation')
- [ ] GO:0061792 secretory granule maturation > not a developmental process/ is_a  GO:0033363 secretory granule organization sufficient (remove LD)
- [ ] GO:0016188 synaptic vesicle maturation  > not a developmental process/ is_a   'vesicle organization' sufficient (remove LD)
- [ ] GO:0060074 'synapse maturation'  not a developmental process is_ GO:0050808 synapse organization sufficient (remove is_a developmental maturation')
- [ ] GO:0048469 cell maturation: move under 'GO:0048869 cellular developmental process'
- [ ] GO:0003006 developmental process involved in reproduction': to be reviewed separately, has many cellular processes
- [ ] GO:0022611 dormancy process: is a developmental process? 
- [ ] GO:0098727 maintenance of cell number: is_a ​​GO:0048871 multicellular organismal-level homeostasis ? 
","createdAt":"2023-06-01T23:41:10Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1572924187","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5dwPRr","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@pgaudet  
CREATE NEW TERM 'multicellular organismal-level developmental process' under which to move organism-level processes (embryonic development?? see https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Animal_embryonic_development)
IS_A developmental process?
If we are to keep the grouping term developmental process, shouldn't cellular developmental process stay? cellular developmental process has dual IS_A parentage.","createdAt":"2023-06-01T23:44:46Z","includesCreatedEdit":true,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1572926571","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5dwUJf","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Perhaps we would keep developmental process, IS_A children include
---cellular developmental process
---multicellular organism-level
---- NTR animal developmental process
----  NTR  plant developmental process
---unicellular organism-level (or merge with cellular)
---cellular component-level","createdAt":"2023-06-02T00:11:38Z","includesCreatedEdit":true,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1572946527","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5eHXeG","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Discussion with @thomaspd and @raymond91125 
- cellular component-level should go under 'organelle biogenesis' (or something like this, depending on the term 
- for now we can keep the top level term ' developmental process', and decide later when looking more broadly at the ontology
- specifically: under ' developmental process', we can keep for now: 

 - cellular developmental process
-  ~multicellular organism-level~
-  NTR animal developmental process
-  NTR  plant developmental process
-   unicellular organism-level (or merge with cellular) (maybe we create later, when we see whether we need it?)
-  cellular process
- -  GO:0032989 cellular component morphogenesis","createdAt":"2023-06-06T15:30:38Z","includesCreatedEdit":true,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1578989446","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5eXFhk","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"New term proposal
animal organism-level developmental process "Any multicellular organismal process whose specific outcome is the progression of a metazoa over time from an initial condition to a later condition." [WIKIPEDIA:Developmental biology]
plant organism-level developmental process "Any multicellular organismal process whose specific outcome is the progression of a  viridiplantae over time from an initial condition to a later condition." [WIKIPEDIA:Developmental biology]
","createdAt":"2023-06-08T18:04:53Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1583110244","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5ebE-h","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Sound good. Could we simplify the labels: 
'animal developmental process' and 'plant developmental process' ? 

Thanks, Pascale","createdAt":"2023-06-09T08:10:08Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1584156577","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5efZZu","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"I just noticed that there is already a term multicellular organism development with LD 'anatomical structure development'

GO currently does not cover these psychological processes.","createdAt":"2023-06-10T00:14:24Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1585299703","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5eonpe","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Suggestion on the ontology call by @cmungall 

---- NTR animal gross anatomical part developmental process
---- NTR plant gross anatomical part developmental process


LD for animal term -> there is no uberon term suitable yet - 'gross anatomical part ' under discussion 

LD for plant term: need to coordinate with PO to create a suitable grouping class","createdAt":"2023-06-12T16:54:34Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1587706462","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5esQb7","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Hi @raymond91125

Is that also what you had in mind? 

Thanks, Pascale","createdAt":"2023-06-13T06:56:41Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1588659963","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5ewP1n","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"I was thinking using something like developmental process as the genus and animal anatomical parts as the differentia. e.g. 'developmental process'

","createdAt":"2023-06-19T09:35:50Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1596846804","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5fOct3","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Yes, I think so. We even have several disease genes like Huntington, or presenilin A and B genes involved in Alzheimer, several genes involved in mitochondrial diseases and many more.","createdAt":"2023-06-19T21:29:21Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1597785927","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5h7Vcj","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Propose to add two new terms as below. +[Term]
+id: GO:0160084
+name: animal gross anatomical part developmental process
+def: "Any developmental process whose specific outcome is the progression of the gross anatomical parts of an animal over time from an initial condition to a later condition." [WIKIPEDIA:Developmental_biology]
+synonym: "animal development" RELATED []
+is_a: GO:0032502 ! developmental process
+property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994" xsd:string
+created_by: rynl
+creation_date: 2023-07-19T23:48:44Z
+scope: biological_process
+
+[Term]
+id: GO:0160085
+name: plant gross anatomical part developmental process
+namespace: biological_process
+def: "Any developmental process whose specific outcome is the progression of the gross anatomical parts of a plant over time from an initial condition to a later condition." [WIKIPEDIA:Developmental_biology]
+synonym: "plant development" RELATED []
+is_a: GO:0032502 ! developmental process
+property_value: term_tracker_item "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994" xsd:string

","createdAt":"2023-07-20T00:44:26Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1642944291","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5iUdvN","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Hi @raymond91125 

I wonder, can we be more specific than "from an initial condition to a later condition" ? This is not what is described in the wikipedia page you cite. Can we have something that mentions that it starts with a zygote and ends with a fully formed/mature organism? 

@ukemi is that too restrictive? 

Thanks, Pascale","createdAt":"2023-07-25T10:11:55Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1649531853","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5iV8iF","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Or maybe to cover specific organ development processes (for example), that start later than at the zygote stage, we could have something that indicates an immature stage to a mature stage? ","createdAt":"2023-07-25T14:09:48Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1649920133","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5iYs8n","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"I thought the goal is to focus on developmental processes of **gross anatomical parts**: such as the heart, the limb, or the nervous system, defined by Uberon for animals; we are not modelling development as temporal progressions of life cycles per se.","createdAt":"2023-07-25T22:22:53Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1650642727","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5ics3o","author":{"login":"pgaudet"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"> we are not modelling development as temporal progressions


But your definition states "progression over time" - I guess this is what I would like to see clarified. We need to be very clear about what these grouping terms are to make sue they only cover the terms we want. 

Maybe "Any developmental process whose specific outcome is the progression of a gross anatomical part of an animal from an initial immature state, to a later, more mature state" ... along these lines? 

Thanks, Pascale","createdAt":"2023-07-26T12:15:21Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1651690984","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5ifKTz","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"The definition I proposed is following the definition we have for the parent term GO:0032502 developmental process. Perhaps the reference can be better but I haven't found one.
The logical definition, when we have a Uberon term would be 'developmental process' and ('results in development of' some 'animal gross anatomical part') ","createdAt":"2023-07-26T19:00:26Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1652335859","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5i33ne","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Ontology editor meeting 2023-07-31:
1. We should specify the initial conditions for terms of specific anatomical parts; an example may be included in the comment of these grouping terms.
2. PO does not have a term of plant gross anatomical part","createdAt":"2023-07-31T17:17:25Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1658812894","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5i8Qx5","author":{"login":"pfey03"},"authorAssociation":"NONE","body":"What about Dicty? https://github.com/dictyBase/migration-data/blob/master/ontologies/dicty_anatomy.obo","createdAt":"2023-08-02T19:23:46Z","includesCreatedEdit":true,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-1662850896","viewerDidAuthor":false},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws5jHW3m","author":{"login":"raymond91125"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"Is DDANAT_0010082 whole organism the proper grouping term equivalent to gross anatomical part in animals or plants? *— @dragon-ai-agent*","createdAt":"2026-05-18T22:49:05Z","includesCreatedEdit":false,"isMinimized":false,"minimizedReason":"","reactionGroups":[],"url":"https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/22994#issuecomment-4482924123","viewerDidAuthor":true},{"id":"IC_kwDOAkAHws8AAAABCzR8sg","author":{"login":"dragon-ai-agent"},"authorAssociation":"MEMBER","body":"@raymond91125 Analysis of developmental process terms that should be `is_a` children of **GO:0160108** (animal gross anatomical part developmental process) or **GO:0160109** (plant gross anatomical part developmental process).

## Method

Both grouping terms describe *"a developmental process whose specific outcome is the progression of a **gross anatomical part** of an animal/plant from an initial immature state to a later, more mature state"*. In GO this is exactly the `anatomical structure development` (GO:0048856) pattern, whose subclasses carry the logical definition:

```
intersection_of: GO:0048856 ! anatomical structure development
intersection_of: results_in_development_of <ANATOMY TERM>
```

I enumerated every non-obsolete term carrying `results_in_development_of` and partitioned by the differentia's source ontology, applying the principle you stated in this thread — *"a cell is not a gross anatomical part"* — to exclude cell-level and subcellular terms.

## Results

| Bucket | Differentia | Count | Disposition |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Animal** | UBERON (multicellular anatomical structure) | **590** | → `is_a` GO:0160108 |
| **Plant** | PO (plant anatomical structure, excl. plant cells) | **61** | → `is_a` GO:0160109 |
| Animal cell development | CL | 99 | *not* gross anatomical part → cellular developmental process |
| Plant cell development | PO (4 plant-cell terms) | 4 | *not* gross anatomical part → cellular developmental process |
| Subcellular | GO CC (dendrite, pollen tube, apical/basal dendrite) | 4 | cellular-component level — out of scope |
| Fungal | FAO (fruitbody, conidium, ascus, basidium, …) | 10 | neither animal nor plant — needs separate grouping |
| Dictyostelium | DDANAT (GO:0048837 sorocarp sorus development) | 1 | neither animal nor plant (cf. @pfey03 discussion) |

`GO:0048856 anatomical structure development` itself (differentia = UBERON:0000061 *anatomical structure*, the root) is the **genus / superclass**, not a child, and is excluded from the 590.

The 4 plant-cell terms excluded from GO:0160109: GO:0010441 guard cell development, GO:0080147 root hair cell development, GO:0090393 sepal giant cell development, GO:0090436 leaf pavement cell development.

## Recommended implementation (no ontology edits made)

This is a "parent relationship query", so I have **not** modified the ontology — asserting ~650 `is_a` edges by hand is unmaintainable and the approach still has open points in this thread. The GO-idiomatic fix is to give the two grouping terms logical definitions and let the reasoner classify all 651 automatically:

```
id: GO:0160108
intersection_of: GO:0048856 ! anatomical structure development
intersection_of: results_in_development_of UBERON:0010000 ! multicellular anatomical structure

id: GO:0160109
intersection_of: GO:0048856 ! anatomical s

synonym: "animal development" RELATED []
